title: lomatium foeniculaceum var. inyoense
intro: Lomatium foeniculaceum var. inyoense, also known as Inyo lomatium or Inyo biscuitroot, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory infections, such as the flu and pneumonia, due to its antiviral and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Lomatium foeniculaceum var. inyoense include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tincture, tea, or capsule, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
